channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump invokes 18th century law to speed deportations, judge stalls it hours later
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge barred the Trump administration Saturday from carrying out deportations under a sweeping 18th century law that the president invoked hours earlier to speed removal of Venezuelan gang members from the United States. U.S. District Judge James E.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump orders strikes on Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen and issues new warning
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump said he ordered a series of airstrikes on the Houthi-held areas in Yemen on Saturday, promising to use “overwhelming lethal force” until Iranian-backed Houthi rebels cease their attacks on shipping along a vital maritime corridor.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, SAMY MAGDY and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump enacts a 1790s law to target ‘alien enemies’ for detention and deportation
item.description: In a presidential action Saturday, Trump invoked the wartime authority Alien Enemies Act, targeting members of the Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua to immediate detention and deportation.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Fetterman claps back at Ocasio-Cortez on government funding vote
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.), who announced days ago that he would vote for a House-passed funding bill to avoid a government shutdown, said he doesn’t care about firebrand Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez’s (D-N.Y.) harsh criticism of Democratic senators who will vote to advance the controversial bill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's pick for hostage negotiations envoy withdraws nomination but will continue on hostage issues
item.description: Adam Boehler, President Donald Trump’s nominee to become special envoy for hostage affairs, has withdrawn his nomination to avoid a requirement that he divest from his investment business.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US prepares to deport about 300 alleged gang members to El Salvador
item.description: El Salvador is agreeing to accept from the United States about 300 alleged members of a Venezuelan gang for one year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: With Trump’s zigzag actions on trade, March came in like a lion and won’t be going out like a lamb
item.description: On the U.S. trade front, March came in like a lion and won’t be going out like a lamb. President Donald Trump has set in motion a tariff spree that expands or changes by the hour.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Transgender sailors, Marines offered benefits to voluntarily leave service or face being kicked out
item.description: The policy aligns with an executive order President Donald Trump signed in January to bar transgender individuals from serving in the military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China's US influence could face crackdown under slate of new bills
item.description: U.S. Sen. James Lankford, R-Okla., rolled out several bills aimed at countering Chinese influence and ownership in the U.S. as tensions grow.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump allies mount campaign to get DOGE codified by Congress
item.description: A bill by Trump's House GOP allies would codify a reporting structure between DOGE and the presidential Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Injunction lifted on Trump executive orders slashing federal DEI support
item.description: An appeals court on Friday overturned a previous judge's decision to block President Donald Trump's executive orders surrounding diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FBI investigating rise in swatting incidents after several conservatives targeted, Kash Patel says
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel on Friday said that the agency is investigating the recent surge in swatting incidents on conservative media figures and others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican legislation seeks to ban Chinese nationals from studying in the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A group of House Republicans on Friday put forward legislation seeking to prevent Chinese students from studying in American schools, as some U.S. lawmakers are targeting China over national security concerns. Rep. Riley Moore, R-W.Va.
item.dc:creator: FU TING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oz pledges to fight health care fraud but makes no commitments on Medicaid funding cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dr. Mehmet Oz promised senators on Friday to fight health care fraud and push to make Americans healthier if he becomes the next leader of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ and TOM MURPHY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate passes HALT Fentanyl Act to permanently classify all fentanyl-related drugs as Schedule I substances
item.description: The Senate passed the HALT Fentanyl Act Friday aimed at closing loopholes exploited by drug traffickers. The bill gained approval from 84 senators, while 16 voted against it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, New York Gov. Hochul discuss Constitution gas pipeline
item.description: Trump said a pipeline would lower the region's energy prices, but it remains uncertain how it could be approved.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: As 'match day' nears, med schools, lawmakers want to expand residencies to keep doctors in Nevada
item.description: On Friday, March 21 — just a week away — more than 40,000 medical students around the country will find out where they will be serving their residencies. About 300 of those graduates are from UNLV, UNR and Touro University, but there aren't enough residencies in the state to keep them here.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former US Sen. Alan Simpson of Wyoming, who bridged partisan gaps with his quick wit, dies at age 93
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — Former U.S. Sen. Alan Simpson, a political legend whose quick wit bridged partisan gaps in the years before today’s political acrimony, has died. He was 93.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oversight Democrats launch review of crypto reserve 'poised to enrich' Trump
item.description: House Oversight Democrats are encouraging President Trump to abandon his plans for a crypto reserve, calling it a strategy to enrich himself at taxpayer expense.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Retired Minnesota flag could receive ‘historic’ designation under new bill
item.description: Some state legislators say it’s important to commemorate the former flag. Others say problematic imagery and the old flag’s depiction of Indigenous Minnesotans is inappropriate.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump brings birthright citizenship argument to Supreme Court
item.description: Trump signed the executive order narrowing birthright citizenship on his first day back in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota House bill to encourage sharing of immigration data with ICE introduced
item.description: The bill aims to prevent state and local governments from limiting their employees’ ability to share immigration data, including the legal status of immigrants, with federal authorities.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Nevada cuts wait time for inmates needing mental health care
item.description: The wait time for inmates needing a mental health bed to regain competency was down to an average 74 days compared to 78 when the 8 News Now Investigators reported on the statewide problem last fall.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Walz warns Department of Education cuts could put students, schools at risk
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz said cuts at the Department of Education could cause budget troubles and confusion at public schools across the state, after the department said it’s cutting half its staff.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Windsor Park deadlines loom, construction yet to start decades after homes began to sink
item.description: About $37 million is allocated to build 93 new homes for people in Windsor Park, a North Las Vegas neighborhood that began sinking decades ago. Now as deadlines are looming, lawmakers learn that construction has yet to begin.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The U.S. agency that monitors weather will cut another 1,000 jobs, AP sources say
item.description: Multiple sources tell The Associated Press that the Trump Administration is starting another round of job cuts — this one more than 1,000 — at the nation’s weather, ocean and fisheries agency.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: EPA signals it could narrow Clean Water Act protections
item.description: The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) has signaled that it could narrow which set of waters receive protections under the Clean Water Act.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Digital license plate bill could include a way for Nevadans to pay registration in installments
item.description: GPS features, no stickers and possibly a way to pay your vehicle registration in installments. Those were some of the reasons that digital license plates might get support at the Nevada Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Overpaid by Social Security? You’ll now owe 100% of the repayment
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – If you accidentally got overpaid through your Social Security benefits, you’ll have to pay 100% of it back starting in late March, the agency announced on Monday. The overpayment withholding rate was previously 10%.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida bill would require some elementary school students to learn cursive
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — A new Florida bill would require some elementary school students to learn cursive writing. The bill, HB 921, would require all public schools to provide cursive writing instruction to students in grades 2 through 5.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mayor Adams’ approval rating reaches record low ahead of election
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – The mayor’s race is heating up just as Mayor Eric Adams’ approval rating hits a new low, according to a new poll from Quinnipiac University. A new poll shows only 20% of voters approve of how Adams is handling his post, while 67% disapprove.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11
